author: Michael J. Meraw
scope: Full manuscript / 37 chapters plus epilogue / 83,779 words
purpose: Machine-readable gate for RevisionGrade evaluation regression tests. An evaluation of this manuscript FAILS if any REQUIRED check is unmet or any FORBIDDEN check triggers. Derived from the Let the River Decide DREAM benchmark, the v2 governed-ledger addendum, and the 9-layer ideal Story Ledger benchmark. Test/benchmark use only; do not inject into production prompts or general platform calibration.

forbidden_unsupported_terms.description: These labels assert facts or framings the manuscript does not support and must not appear in evaluation output without direct manuscript citation.
forbidden_unsupported_terms.gate_behavior: CANON_GROUNDING_ERROR — evaluation must be rejected if any forbidden term appears. The front matter signals fictionalization; the evaluator must not assert documentary or memoir status, and must not assert that the Tłekeh Dene community, its protocol, or its ceremonies are real-world entities.

cultural_protocol_gate.description: Mandatory, manuscript-specific safety gate. This manuscript depicts a fictionalized northern Indigenous (Tłekeh Dene) community, invented protocol, ceremony, elders, food covenant, and spiritual belief narrated by an outsider.
required_behavior: Evaluation MUST include an explicit cultural/protocol risk analysis as a first-class section, not a footnote.
Evaluation MUST recommend a qualified Indigenous (northern / Dene-adjacent) sensitivity reader before publication.
Evaluation MUST treat 'witness vs ownership' as a structural craft problem, not only a theme.
Evaluation MUST NOT certify the manuscript as publication-ready while cultural/protocol review is outstanding.
cultural_protocol_gate.gate_note: If the cultural/protocol risk section is absent, the evaluation is a hard test failure regardless of other scores. Readiness MUST NOT be scored above 65 while review is outstanding.

required_entities.description: Tiered treatment requirements. Each tier must be satisfied independently; meeting a lower tier does not satisfy a higher tier.

entities.tier_2_role: First-person witness narrator; outsider traveling north; carries the witness-vs-ownership ethical pressure; road-family son figure to Cliff
entities.tier_5_recommendation_check: Do not recommend 'adding a narrator' or 'establishing a POV.' Mike is the established first-person narrator. Recommend sharpening the witness/ownership boundary only.

entities.tier_2_role: Stepfather / road-family anchor; aging-competence figure; skeptic who undergoes a belief transition from humoring Mike to witnessing the river; comic and tender counterweight
entities.tier_5_recommendation_check: Do not recommend 'adding a companion character.' Cliff is present and load-bearing. Recommend making his belief shift visible and earned only.

entities.tier_2_role: Dogs as sentinels / kin / nonhuman witness; instinct and mortality; their behavior changes stakes and signals the river's activity
entities.tier_5_recommendation_check: Do not treat the dogs as decorative pet material. They are a functional animal-sensory layer. Do not recommend 'removing' or 'reducing' them as flavor.
entities.warning: The dogs are sensory EVIDENCE, not charm. An evaluation that reduces them to atmosphere is a STRUCTURAL omission.

entities.tier_2_role: Elder; authority on river protocol and Smokehouse Camp; gatekeeper of story and knowledge; source of the witness-vs-ownership boundary ('You cannot carry our stories')
entities.tier_5_recommendation_check: Do not recommend 'adding an elder.' William is present. Do not flatten him into a vessel for sacred authority; recommend ordinary-personhood texture only.
entities.warning: Risk flag: William can read as a container for sacred authority rather than a fully ordinary person. Evaluation must note this representational risk.

entities.tier_2_role: Divided-inheritance figure; corporate hinge (Verdant); embodies conflict inside inheritance; prevents a simplistic Indigenous-good / corporate-bad binary; late-arriving central antagonist / sacrifice figure
entities.tier_5_recommendation_check: Do not recommend 'adding a corporate antagonist.' Leanna is present. Recommend seeding her earlier (before Ch. 31) only.
entities.warning: Leanna arrives too late structurally. Evaluation must flag late integration but MUST NOT recommend 'creating' her.

entities.tier_2_role: Community figure; giver of stone/feather offerings; carries grief and protocol; embodies food/work/social density of Smokehouse Camp
entities.warning: Risk flag: Esmé can read as a container for sacred authority rather than an ordinary person. Evaluation must note representational risk.

entities.tier_2_role: Institutional antagonist system; corporate/legal extraction force; euphemism and 'extraction spellwork' embodied in documents
Community figure whose status (inheritance / exclusion / witness / successor) is an unresolved promise requiring payoff
entities.warning: Anthony's status is an unresolved promise. Evaluation must track it, not invent a resolution.

required_relationships.description: These relationship spines must be evaluated, not merely mentioned. Each has a minimum treatment level.

relationships.function: Road family / father-figure bond; aging-competence tenderness and banter; emotional spine of the road narrative
relationships.minimum_treatment: Must be evaluated as a structural spine with criterion-level impact on Character Depth and Tonal Authority
relationships.gate_note: If the Mike–Cliff spine is omitted from a commercial pitch or summary, that is a STRUCTURAL omission of the book's load-bearing emotional arc.

relationships.function: Witness / judgment / memory; the river as active agent, archive, and judge — not setting or mood
relationships.minimum_treatment: Must treat the river as an agentive symbolic force whose agency and ambiguity are protected, not as scenery
relationships.gate_note: An evaluation that treats the river only as setting/atmosphere is a SYMBOLIC_SYSTEM_OMISSION. The river is the title's argument.

relationships.function: Outsider access to story/ceremony; the central ethical boundary ('You cannot carry our stories')
relationships.minimum_treatment: Must identify the witness-vs-ownership boundary as a structural craft problem and check whether it has narrative consequence (refusal, correction, silence, limit)

relationships.function: Corporate inheritance hinge; conflict inside inheritance that breaks the Indigenous-good / corporate-bad binary
relationships.minimum_treatment: Must identify Leanna as the divided-inheritance figure and flag her late structural arrival

relationships.function: Animal-sensory witness layer; the dogs register the river's activity before humans do
relationships.minimum_treatment: Must treat the dogs as functional sensory evidence, not decorative companions

relationships.function: Inheritance / settlement / war / silence; epilogue family-current layer that must connect to river ethics
relationships.minimum_treatment: Must note the Plunkett inheritance layer and whether it integrates with the river's witness ethic

required_object_systems.description: These objects/systems must be evaluated as functional elements of the manuscript's symbolic system, not merely named.

objects.function: Memory, judge, archive, body, nervous system; the central symbolic and structural engine; the title's argument
objects.minimum_treatment: Must be evaluated as an agentive symbolic SYSTEM whose power and ambiguity must be protected; must not be flattened to setting

objects.function: Ominous marker / surveillance / corporate-or-unknown threat; thriller hook introduced with heavy opening weight
objects.minimum_treatment: Must identify NV115 as an underpaid promise relative to its opening weight; must flag the need to resolve or intentionally reframe (criminal threat / corporate surveillance / river-marked omen / misread paranoia)
objects.note: Promise/payoff gate: NV115 is over-weighted at open and under-paid later. Recommend resolution or deliberate reframe, not removal.

objects.function: Covenant, kinship, safe offering, human/dog equality; embodied protocol of Smokehouse Camp
objects.minimum_treatment: Must be evaluated as a covenant/kinship system, not a quaint scene; must remain embodied, not overexplained

objects.function: Indigenous archive / non-paper authority / counter-document opposing corporate and legal papers
objects.minimum_treatment: Must be evaluated as a counter-document system set against corporate/legal documents

objects.function: Legal fiction / euphemism / 'extraction spellwork'; a world-building and antagonist system that must drive plot, not only critique
objects.minimum_treatment: Must evaluate corporate/legal language as a world-building system, and flag where evidence accumulation becomes pacing drag

objects.function: Memory, verdict, offering, grief marker, witness; returned grief and sky/water connection
objects.minimum_treatment: Must track stones/feathers as witness-and-consequence offerings with personal meaning for Cliff/Mike

required_structural_lanes.description: These structural lanes must be represented in the evaluation. Missing an entire lane is a STRUCTURAL_LANE_OMISSION failure.

closure_score_governance.description: Scoring governance rules for the title-bearing closure and the open promises.
closure_score_governance.rule: The final 'voice for the river' question is the title payoff and must be recognized as a strong, intentional close. Narrative Closure MUST NOT be scored below 6/10 solely because plot threads remain open by design.
closure_score_governance.corollary: A Closure score of 7/10 or higher requires the evaluation to account for the open promises (NV115, Anthony's status, Plunkett integration) as a documented promise/payoff gap, not to pretend they are resolved.

recommendation_already_present_guard.description: Any recommendation to 'add,' 'introduce,' or 'create' the following elements is a RECOMMENDATION_ERROR. These elements are already present in the manuscript.
already_present: Fritz and Schultz (the dogs) as sentinels
Verdant / OCEA as corporate antagonist system
The river as agentive symbolic force
The final 'voice for the river' question

revise_queue_protections.description: Rules for what may and may not enter the Revise queue from an evaluation of this manuscript.
blocked_from_revise_queue: Any task asserting memoir / non-fiction / true-story status
Any task recommending 'add a narrator' or 'establish a POV' (Mike is present)
Any task recommending 'add a corporate antagonist' (Leanna / Verdant are present)
Any task recommending 'remove the dogs' or treating them as decorative
Any task recommending 'add an elder' (William is present)
Any task that certifies publication readiness while cultural/protocol review is outstanding
required_revise_queue_entries: Compress the evidence ledger by 15-25% without losing scale
Seed Leanna / Verdant before Chapter 31 (memory, photo, document, news clip, or William's avoidance)
Clarify NV115's function (criminal threat / corporate surveillance / river-marked omen / misread paranoia)
Run a witness/ownership revision pass that dramatizes refusal, correction, silence, or consequence
Make Anthony's status pay off (inheritance, exclusion, witness, or successor function)
Make the council's non-signature materially consequential
Commission an Indigenous (northern / Dene-adjacent) sensitivity reader before publication

ci_acceptance_gate.description: A future evaluation of Let the River Decide PASSES this fixture if ALL of the following return true.

acceptable_score_delta.description: A future evaluation passes if its scores fall within this delta from the truth target.

known_failure_specimen.label: hypothetical_offending_evaluation_v1
known_failure_specimen.note: No production offending run exists for this manuscript. This specimen encodes the anticipated failure modes documented in the DREAM benchmark and v2 governed-ledger addendum, for use as a negative training example.
critical_misses: River treated as setting/atmosphere rather than agentive judge/archive
Dogs (Fritz and Schultz) reduced to decorative pet material
Smokehouse Camp flattened into generic Indigenous-community setting
Leanna omitted or treated only as late minor character, not divided-inheritance hinge
Witness-vs-ownership boundary treated as theme only, not structural craft problem

largest_criterion_gap.cause: Symbolic-system blindness — river agency and witness/ownership boundary read as mood and message rather than the manuscript's load-bearing structural argument
